findings: Connection status building could crash with partial diagnostics payloads.
Device-auth retry-after parsing did not support compact duration notation like '2m 30s'.
Auth cooldown calculations could raise timezone mismatch errors with naive/aware datetime values.
risks: No origin remote is configured in this workspace, so PR URL and merge automation cannot be verified locally.
Only targeted Studio API/connection tests were run; full-suite regressions remain unverified.
changes: Hardened connection status DTO construction against missing diagnostic keys.
Added compact retry-after parser support for h/m/s duration tokens.
Normalized datetime timezone handling in cooldown calculations and added coverage tests.

test_results.command: uv run --with ruff ruff check substrate scripts tests
uv run python -m compileall substrate scripts

uv run --with ruff ruff check substrate scripts tests
uv run python -m compileall substrate scripts
PYTHONPATH=. uv run --with pytest --with httpx pytest -q tests/studio/test_connection.py tests/studio/test_api.py tests/studio/test_connection_service.py

assumptions: Cycle execution was treated as three focused reliability/security-tooling hardening iterations over Studio connection surfaces.
Branch/PR automation was constrained by missing git remote configuration in this environment.
next_tasks: Owner: bot | Add end-to-end tests for device-auth parsing in main API flow | Acceptance: /api/connection/device-auth/start returns retry_after_seconds for compact durations.
Owner: human | Configure origin remote and GitHub auth in CI runner | Acceptance: autonomous branch push and draft PR creation succeed non-interactively.
Owner: bot | Run full PYTHONPATH=. uv run --with pytest --with httpx pytest -q suite before release tagging | Acceptance: all tests pass and evidence is recorded in artifacts.
